company.name: AI4Comms
company.description: AI4comms helps communications leaders turn AI from abstract hype into practical advantage through strategy, training, governance, and hands-on implementation support.

services.name: AI Strategy for Communications Teams
services.description: Strategic advisory for communication leaders who need to define where AI creates real value across corporate communications, public affairs, media relations, and content operations.

services.name: AI Training and Team Enablement
services.description: Practical training programs that help communications teams adopt AI tools responsibly, improve workflows, and build confidence without drowning in jargon.

services.name: Governance and Responsible AI for Comms
services.description: Policy, governance, and operating-model support for organizations that need to use AI in communications without creating reputational, legal, or workflow risk.

services.name: Executive AI Advisory
services.description: Senior advisory support for leaders who need a clear view on how AI is reshaping communications functions, stakeholder expectations, and team structures.

credentials._note: pendingTruth: AI4Comms shares the Duke B.V.; confirm whether it presents Duke's memberships (e.g. Logeion) as its own.

publicModels.name: Advisory Retainer or Project Fee
publicModels.description: Used for strategic advisory, governance work, and executive support. Pricing depends on scope, access, and pace.

publicModels.name: Workshop and Enablement Fee
publicModels.description: Used for training sessions, team enablement, and playbook development. Pricing depends on team size and preparation required.

people.name: Ingo Heijnen
people.title: Co-founder
people.publicBio: Ingo Heijnen is co-founder of AI4Comms. He spent more than 25 years in corporate communications and corporate affairs, most of it at Hill & Knowlton, advising executives and building senior communications teams. He now focuses on making AI practically useful for the communications function.
people.quoteStyle: Measured and senior; speaks from decades of in-the-room experience and frames AI as a strategic accelerator, never as hype.

people.name: Emma van Gelder
people.title: Co-founder
people.publicBio: Emma van Gelder is a co-founder of AI4Comms. She worked in corporate communications at Hill & Knowlton before co-founding AI4Comms, where she focuses on helping organisations treat AI adoption in communications as a leadership challenge rather than a tooling question.
people.quoteStyle: Mission-driven and collaborative; frames AI adoption in communications as a leadership question, not a tooling one.

people.name: Ariën Stuijt
people.title: Advisor
people.publicBio: Ariën Stuijt is a former journalist (Dow Jones Newswires, The Wall Street Journal) who spent 17 years in strategic consulting at Hill & Knowlton, specialising in transformative situations such as crises, shareholder activism, M&A, and stakeholder communication. He advises on keeping authority and authenticity in the age of generative AI.
people.quoteStyle: Reflective and principled; moves between corporate-affairs analysis and public commentary on journalism and the rule of law; writes in Dutch and English.
